I tried to use linalg inv for a batch of small random matrices with streams. I observed the error that the outputs are filled with NANs and infs. 

In the code snipped, the inverse of the identity matrix is taken. In the first call, the output is correct, but not in the second call.
When rng is commented out the error does not occur. In addition, the error does not occur with additional device synchronization.

![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/86b5ce82-f7db-402a-95d6-0c6332bde686)

### To Reproduce

```py
import cupy as cp

def test_inv_stream(
    matrix_size,
    batch_size,
    dtype
):
    rng = cp.random.default_rng(seed=1)

    a_d = cp.empty((batch_size, matrix_size, matrix_size), dtype=dtype)

    # increase numerical stability
    for i in range(batch_size):
        a_d[i] = cp.eye(matrix_size, dtype=dtype)

    stream1 = cp.cuda.stream.Stream(non_blocking=True)
    
    stream1.synchronize()
    with stream1:
        inv_ref_d = cp.linalg.inv(a_d)
    stream1.synchronize()
    print(inv_ref_d)

test_inv_stream(5, 1, cp.float64)
test_inv_stream(10, 1, cp.float64)
```

Wheel (`pip install cupy-***`)

Describe your issue.

When dividing a `scipy.sparse.lil_array` by a complex number, and subsequently trying to convert it to `scipy.sparse.csr_array`, a `TypeError` is raised during the conversion.

The same does not happen for other sparse matrix types.

### Reproducing Code Example

```python
from scipy import sparse

matrix = sparse.random(10, 10, 0.1, format="lil")
matrix = matrix / 1j
matrix.tocsr()


The above example results in an error, while the following works as expected:
```python
from scipy import sparse

matrix = sparse.random(10, 10, 0.1, format="coo")
matrix = matrix / 1j
matrix.tocsr()
```
```

#### What does this implement/fix?
<!--Please explain your changes.-->
This PR adds an `overlap` parameter to `scipy.linalg.block_diag`. This allows one to specify a number of rows/cols that the matrices should overlap additively. I found this piece of code very useful when constructing finite-element system-matrices from the matrices of coupled periodic sub-domains.


#### Additional information
<!--Any additional information you think is important.-->
I realize the use-cases for this parameter may be very limited and specific to my problem, but I could not find a solution in any other package out there and think others could find it useful as well.

It may even be interesting to add an option to specify the type of operation that should be performed on overlapping elements but that may be overkill.
